stories.headline: Apple Rebuilds Siri as “Siri AI” on a Custom Google Gemini Model
stories.key_takeaway: Apple has rebuilt Siri on a custom 1.2-trillion-parameter Gemini model at a reported $1B a year, the most consequential AI bet in the company’s history and one that invites deep antitrust scrutiny over its growing Google dependency.

pull_quote.text: We partnered deeply with Google to co-create the next generation of Apple Foundation Models.

stories.article_excerpt: Apple rebuilt Siri from the ground up on a custom 1.2-trillion-parameter Gemini model, paying roughly $1B a year.

stories.headline: Google Releases Gemma 4 Open Models Built for Reasoning and Agentic Workflows
stories.key_takeaway: Google’s Gemma 4 12B brings capable multimodal reasoning, a 256K context window and 80-percent-plus coding scores to any machine with 16 GB of RAM, all under a permissive Apache 2.0 license.

pull_quote.text: Gemma 4 12B is the most capable model we have ever put in that footprint.

stories.article_excerpt: Google DeepMind shipped two new open models on June 3 — a 12B dense model that runs on a standard laptop.

stories.headline: Google Confirms Gemini 3.5 Pro Arriving Next Month
stories.key_takeaway: Google has committed to a July 2026 release window for Gemini 3.5 Pro, a flagship that must out-reason GPT-5.5 and Claude Opus 4.8 while also powering Apple’s newly rebuilt Siri at scale.

pull_quote.text: Give us until next month to get it to you.

stories.article_excerpt: Google set a July 2026 window for Gemini 3.5 Pro as the frontier race intensifies on every front.

stories.headline: Alibaba Ships Qwen3 Coder Next as a New Open-Weight Coding Model
stories.key_takeaway: Alibaba’s Qwen3 Coder Next uses an ultra-sparse 80B / 3B-active mixture-of-experts design to top 70 percent on SWE-Bench Verified under Apache 2.0, extending China’s open-weight push toward the frontier.

pull_quote.text: Qwen3-Coder-Next is a specialized 80-billion-parameter model designed to deliver elite agentic performance within a lightweight active footprint.

stories.article_excerpt: China’s Alibaba deepened its open-weight coding bet with Qwen3 Coder Next, squeezing near-frontier scores from a sparse model.

stories.headline: Suno Raises $400M Series D at a $5.4B Valuation as Label Lawsuits Mount
stories.key_takeaway: Suno’s $400M Series D at a $5.4B valuation cements its lead in AI music, but the bet hinges on whether Warner’s licensing settlement becomes a template before Universal and Sony’s 61,000-recording lawsuit produces a ruinous ruling.

pull_quote.text: This funding will help us accelerate what matters most: helping more people express themselves through music.

stories.article_excerpt: The AI music startup that turns prompts into radio-ready tracks is now one of the most valuable and most contested companies in entertainment tech.

stories.headline: Generalist AI Raises $400M at a $2B Valuation to Build “Physical AGI”
stories.key_takeaway: Generalist AI’s $400M raise at a $2B valuation, backed by Nvidia and a roster of AI luminaries, is the largest single bet yet that a universal foundation model, not hardware, will own the intelligence layer of robotics.

pull_quote.text: It starts to cross in a general way into commercial viability for very simple tasks.

stories.article_excerpt: The startup that wants to put a universal brain inside every robot just raised the biggest bet yet that such a brain is achievable, and soon.

stories.headline: Flourish Raises $500M to Build Brain-Inspired AI With Bezos and Google Backing
stories.key_takeaway: Flourish raised $500M at a reported $2.5B valuation to build brain-inspired Cortex AI targeting 50-watt efficiency, backed by Jeff Bezos, GV and Lux Capital, the most prominent bet yet in post-transformer foundational AI.

pull_quote.text: A synthetic artificial intelligence brain that runs on 50 watts or less.

stories.article_excerpt: A secretive New York startup wants to reverse-engineer the human brain and run the result on 50 watts. Half a billion dollars says it might work.

stories.headline: PhysicsX Raises $300M Series C Led by Temasek to Scale Engineering AI
stories.key_takeaway: PhysicsX’s $300M Series C at a $2.4B valuation, led by Temasek with Nvidia and Siemens returning, confirms that AI-native physics simulation has crossed from research novelty to industrial necessity.

pull_quote.text: We are giving engineers the ability to explore thousands of designs where they once managed a handful, in seconds rather than weeks.

stories.article_excerpt: A London startup that shrinks engineering simulations from days to seconds just hit a $2.4 billion valuation.

stories.headline: Meta Cuts About 8,000 Jobs in an AI-Focused Restructuring
stories.key_takeaway: Meta is cutting roughly 8,000 jobs and reassigning 7,000 more to AI teams to free capital for an AI infrastructure plan that could reach $145 billion in 2026.

pull_quote.text: We basically have two major cost centers in the company: compute infrastructure and people-oriented things.

stories.article_excerpt: Meta employees across Asia received emails telling them their jobs were gone as the company reorganizes around AI.

stories.headline: OpenAI Launches a “Deployment Company” to Help Enterprises Build Around Its Models
stories.key_takeaway: OpenAI launched a majority-owned, roughly $4 billion deployment subsidiary that embeds forward-deployed engineers inside enterprises, opening a direct services contest with Anthropic’s partner-ecosystem approach.

pull_quote.text: The challenge now is helping companies integrate these systems into the infrastructure and workflows that power their businesses.

stories.article_excerpt: With more than a million businesses using its products, OpenAI’s next problem is not a better model, it is helping customers actually use it.

stories.headline: Google’s TurboQuant Slashes LLM Memory Overhead at ICLR 2026
stories.key_takeaway: Google’s TurboQuant compresses LLM key-value cache entries to about 3 bits with near-zero quality loss, cutting memory up to 6x and speeding H100 attention up to 8x, all without retraining.

pull_quote.text: TurboQuant operates within a factor of approximately 2.7 of the information-theoretic compression limit.

stories.article_excerpt: Every time a language model generates a token it silently runs up a memory bill. TurboQuant shrinks it.

stories.headline: UPenn’s “Mollifier Layers” Bring Classical Math Into Neural Networks to Solve Inverse PDEs
stories.key_takeaway: Penn researchers replaced unstable recursive autodiff in physics-informed neural networks with a convolutional mollifier layer drawn from 1940s mathematics, yielding more accurate and noise-robust recovery of hidden parameters in inverse PDE problems.

pull_quote.text: Modern AI often advances by scaling up computation. But some scientific challenges require better mathematics, not just more compute.

stories.article_excerpt: Watch ripples cross a pond and calculate exactly where a pebble struck. That is the inverse problem these layers tackle.

stories.headline: Generative-Physics Hybrid Runs Climate Models About 25x Faster
stories.key_takeaway: UC San Diego and the Allen Institute for AI built Spherical DYffusion, a generative-diffusion climate emulator that runs century-long simulations about 25 times faster than state-of-the-art physics models while staying competitive on accuracy.

pull_quote.text: We emulated the atmosphere, which is one of the most important elements in a climate model.

stories.article_excerpt: A model called Spherical DYffusion can project a century of climate in a single day, work that once took supercomputers weeks.

stories.headline: A Deep Learning System Called MycoBCP Spots Subtle Tuberculosis Cell Changes
stories.key_takeaway: MycoBCP, a Gates-Foundation-funded deep learning platform from UC San Diego and Linnaeus Bioscience, identified the mechanism of TB-targeting compounds in 96 percent of blind tests, speeding a key bottleneck in tuberculosis drug discovery.

pull_quote.text: This is the first time that this kind of image analysis using machine learning and AI has been applied in this way to bacteria.
pull_quote.role: Professor of Molecular Biology, UC San Diego

stories.article_excerpt: Facing a wall of fluorescence-microscopy images of TB cells, researchers taught a model to read what humans could not.

stories.headline: Microsoft Open Source Tools Hacked to Steal AI Developers’ Passwords
stories.key_takeaway: A self-replicating worm exploited un-rotated GitHub Actions secrets to compromise dozens of Microsoft Azure repositories, stealing developer credentials through malicious configs that fired the moment a repo was opened in AI coding agents like Claude Code and Gemini CLI.

pull_quote.text: We have temporarily removed some repositories as we investigated potential malicious content.

stories.article_excerpt: The malware did not wait for you to run any code. It fired the moment you opened the repository.

stories.headline: US and Japan Commit $1 Billion to a Joint AI Research Effort Through the DOE
stories.key_takeaway: The Department of Energy’s $1 billion, five-year Genesis Mission partnership with Japan, the first international deal of its kind, links a dozen US national labs with Japanese institutions to accelerate AI-driven breakthroughs in fusion, quantum, materials and biotech.

pull_quote.text: This partnership brings together two of the world’s great scientific powers to accelerate discovery and unlock breakthroughs that will shape the future.
pull_quote.role: Under Secretary for Science, US DOE

stories.article_excerpt: The US and Japan announced a $1 billion partnership to pursue AI-driven science together, with Japan as the first Genesis Mission partner.

stories.headline: White House and Congress Relaunch a Push to Preempt State AI Laws
stories.key_takeaway: The White House and key senators are negotiating to pair federal preemption of state AI laws with kids-online-safety and anti-deepfake bills, reviving a fight Congress has twice rejected, just as Colorado’s enforcement deadline looms.

pull_quote.text: This bill takes the current floor on state AI legislation and turns it into a federal ceiling.

stories.article_excerpt: With Colorado’s landmark AI law weeks from enforcement, Washington is reviving a twice-rejected bid to override state rules.
